title: ASEP Engine Manifest
description: Declarative boundary contract for an engine. Engines are the platform's units of capability (generation, conversion, upgrade, audit, integration). A manifest declares an engine's identity, contract, and lifecycle status WITHOUT prescribing implementation. Engines MUST NOT be tightly coupled to one another.

name.description: Engine name in the canonical language.

capability.description: The single primary capability class this engine provides.

status.description: Lifecycle status. Phase 1 engines are boundaries only.

summary.description: One-line description of the engine's responsibility.

contract.description: The engine's boundary: what it consumes and what it produces. Both are described by kind, never by implementation.

sideEffects.description: Declared side effects. REQUIRED so effects are always explicit (JSON Schema 'default' is an annotation and is not applied by validators). Safe-by-default: an engine with no external effects MUST declare ["none"]; destructive or external effects MUST be listed and are disabled unless explicitly enabled.

dependsOn.description: Other engines this engine depends on, by stable ID. Dependencies MUST form a directed acyclic graph.

kind.description: Logical type of data crossing the boundary.
